collectively referred to as 'RCBs' / 'banks' and individually as 'RCB' / 'bank').

requirements.full_sentence:

specified below, to the extent such activities are undertaken by it.

requirements.full_sentence:

requirements.full_sentence:
Due diligence requirements with respect to LSPs
(1) Digital lending by a bank involving a LSP, shall be carried out under a contractual
agreement between the bank and the LSP, which clearly defines the respective
roles, rights, and obligations of each party thereto.
requirements.object: a contractual agreement between the bank and the LSP, which clearly defines the respective roles, rights, and obligations of each

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall conduct enhanced due diligence before they enter into an
agreement with a LSP for digital lending, taking into account LSP's technical
capabilities, robustness of data privacy policies and storage systems, fairness
in conduct with borrowers, past records of conduct and ability to comply with all
applicable regulations and statutes.
requirements.object: before they enter into an agreement with a LSP for digital lending, taking into account LSP's technical capabilities, r

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall carry out periodic review of the conduct of the LSP vis-à-vis the
terms of the contractual agreement and shall take appropriate action in the event
of any deviation therefrom.
requirements.object: of the conduct of the LSP vis-à-vis the terms of the contractual agreement and shall take appropriate action in the event of

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A bank shall lay down, as part of its policy, suitable monitoring mechanisms for
the loan portfolios originated with the support of LSPs.
requirements.object: of its policy, suitable monitoring mechanisms for the loan portfolios originated with the support of LSPs

requirements.full_sentence:

discharge their duties responsibly and ensure that LSP complies with the

instructions as issued from time to time.
requirements.object: LSP acting as a recovery agent, to discharge their duties responsibly and ensure that LSP complies with the applicable in

specified under the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks -
Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025 and shall ensure that the LSPs
engaged by them and the DLAs (either of the bank or of the LSPs engaged by
the bank) comply with these Directions.
requirements.object: the extant guidelines on outsourcing as specified under the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Managing Risks in

with the lender(s) to offer a loan, it shall follow a consistent approach for similarly
placed borrowers and products.

subsequent changes to this mechanism shall be properly documented.

Assessing the borrower's creditworthiness
(1) A bank shall obtain the necessary information relating to economic profile of the
borrower with a view to assessing the borrower's creditworthiness before
extending any loan, including, at a minimum, age, occupation and income
details.

(1) A bank shall provide a Key Fact Statement (KFS), as per instructions contained

requirements.object: Statement (KFS), as per instructions contained in the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Responsible Business Conduc

requirements.full_sentence:
Loan disbursal, servicing and repayment
(1) Disbursement of loan by the bank shall always be made into the bank account
of the borrower except for disbursals covered exclusively under statutory or
regulatory mandate (of the Reserve Bank or of any other regulator) and
disbursals for specific end use, provided the loan is disbursed directly into the
bank account of the end-beneficiary.
requirements.object: the bank account of the borrower except for disbursals covered exclusively under statutory or regulatory mandate (of the Reserve Bank or of any other regulator) and disbursals for specific end use

Provided that advances against salary, where the loan is disbursed directly to
the bank account of the borrower, but the repayment is from the corporate
employer, can be allowed subject to the condition that the loan is repaid by the

requirements.full_sentence: (4) A bank shall ensure that any fees, charges, reimbursements, etc.

reflected in full in the borrower's account on the same day and bank shall ensure

requirements.full_sentence:
Cooling-off period
(1) The borrower shall be given an explicit option to exit a digital loan by paying the
principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty during an initial
"cooling-off period".
requirements.object: option to exit a digital loan by paying the principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty during an initial "cooling-of

The cooling off period shall be determined by the Board of
the bank in terms of their credit policy, subject to the period so determined not
being less than one day.
requirements.object: Board of the bank in terms of their credit policy

requirements.full_sentence:
For borrower continuing with the loan even after
cooling-off period, pre-payment shall continue to be allowed as per the Reserve
Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Responsible Business Conduct)
Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as per the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank may retain a reasonable one-time processing fee, if the customer exits
the loan during the cooling-off period.

(1) A bank, and the LSP which has an interface with the borrower, shall designate

requirements.full_sentence:
Storage of data
(1) A bank shall ensure that LSP engaged by them do not store personal
information of borrower except some basic minimal data (viz., name, address,
contact details of the customer, etc.) that may be required to carry out their
operations or service within the scope of the bank-LSP agreement.

requirements.full_sentence:
Responsibility regarding data privacy and security of the customer's personal
information on an ongoing basis shall be that of the bank.
requirements.object: bank

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall ensure that clear policy guidelines regarding the storage of
customer data including the type of data that can be stored, the length of time
for which data can be stored, restrictions on the use of data, data destruction
protocol, standards for handling security breach, etc., are put in place and also
disclosed by the bank and the LSP engaged by the bank prominently on their
website and DLA at all times.
requirements.object: guidelines regarding the storage of customer data including the type of data that can be stored, the length of time for which

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank shall ensure that no biometric data is stored/ collected by the bank and
LSP, unless allowed under extant statutory guidelines.
requirements.object: data is stored/ collected by the bank and LSP

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A bank shall ensure that all data is stored only in servers located within India,
while ensuring compliance with statutory obligations/ regulatory instructions.
requirements.object: is stored only in servers located within India, while ensuring compliance with statutory obligations/ regulatory instructions

conditions: in case the data is processed outside India
requirements.full_sentence:
Further, in case the data is processed outside India, the same shall be deleted
from servers outside India and brought back to India within 24 hours of
processing.
requirements.object: outside India and brought back to India within 24 hours of processing

requirements.full_sentence:
Comprehensive privacy policy
(1) A bank and LSPs engaged by bank shall have a comprehensive privacy policy
compliant with applicable laws, associated regulations and RBI guidelines which
shall be made available publicly on the website of bank and LSP, as the case
may be.
requirements.object: policy compliant with applicable laws, associated regulations and RBI guidelines which shall be made available publicly o

requirements.object: the privacy policy

requirements.full_sentence:
Technology standards
(1) A bank shall ensure that they and the LSPs engaged by them comply with
various technology standards/ requirements on cybersecurity stipulated by RBI
and other relevant agencies, or as may be specified from time to time, for
undertaking digital lending.

A bank
shall ensure that LSPs, if any, associated with such deferred payment credit

those of the LSPs, either exclusively or as a platform participant, on the

Bank in the requisite format as given in the Annex-I to these Directions.

Board of the bank for the purpose shall certify that the data on DLAs submitted

requirements.full_sentence:
Eligibility as DLG provider
(1) A bank may enter into DLG arrangements only with an LSP/ other lender
engaged as an LSP.
requirements.object: only with an LSP/ other lender engaged as an LSP

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the LSP providing DLG shall be incorporated as a
company under the Companies Act, 2013.
requirements.object: company under the Companies Act, 2013

conditions: if any, payable to/ received by the DLG provider
requirements.full_sentence:
Due diligence and other requirements with respect to DLG provider
(1) A bank, including a bank acting as DLG provider, shall lay down, as part of its
policy, the eligibility criteria for DLG provider, nature and extent of DLG cover,
process of monitoring and reviewing the DLG arrangement, and the details of
the fees, if any, payable to/ received by the DLG provider, as the case may be
before entering into any DLG arrangement.
requirements.object: of its policy, the eligibility criteria for DLG provider, nature and extent of DLG cover, process of monitoring and reviewing the DLG arrangement, and the details of the fees

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall ensure that any DLG arrangement does not act as a substitute for
credit appraisal requirements and robust credit underwriting standards need to
be put in place irrespective of the DLG cover.
requirements.object: arrangement does not act as a substitute for credit appraisal requirements and robust credit underwriting standards need to be put

requirements.full_sentence:

adequate information to satisfy itself that the entity extending DLG would be able
to honour it.
requirements.object: satisfy itself that the entity extending DLG would be able to honour it

requirements.full_sentence:
Such information shall, at a minimum, include a declaration from

number of portfolios against which DLG has been provided.
requirements.object: include a declaration from the DLG provider, certified by the statutory auditor of the DLG provider, on the aggregate DLG amount outst

requirements.full_sentence:
The declaration
shall also contain past default rates on similar portfolios.
requirements.object: rates on similar portfolios

requirements.full_sentence:
Restrictions on entering into DLG arrangements
(1) A bank shall not enter into DLG arrangements for revolving credit facilities
offered through digital lending channel.
requirements.object: for revolving credit facilities offered through digital lending channel

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank shall not enter into DLG arrangements on the loans which are covered
by the credit guarantee schemes administered by trust funds as specified under
the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Prudential Norms on
Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: on the loans which are covered by the credit guarantee schemes administered by trust funds as specified under the Reserve

requirements.full_sentence:
Structure of DLG arrangements
(1) DLG arrangements shall be backed by an explicit and legally enforceable
contract between the bank and the DLG provider.
requirements.object: explicit and legally enforceable contract between the bank and the DLG provider

requirements.full_sentence:
Such contract, among other
things, shall contain the following details:

Forms of DLG
(1) A bank shall accept DLG only in one or more of the following forms:
requirements.object: one or more of the following forms:

requirements.full_sentence:
Cap on DLG
(1) A bank shall ensure that the total amount of DLG cover on any outstanding
portfolio which is specified upfront shall not exceed five per cent of the total
amount disbursed out of that loan portfolio at any given time.
requirements.object: of the total amount disbursed out of that loan portfolio at any given time

conditions:
In case of implicit
guarantee arrangements
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of implicit
guarantee arrangements, the DLG Provider shall not bear performance risk of
more than the equivalent amount of five per cent of the underlying loan portfolio.
requirements.object: more than the equivalent amount of five per cent of the underlying loan portfolio

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The portfolio over which DLG can be offered shall consist of identifiable and
measurable loan assets which have been sanctioned (the 'DLG set').
requirements.object: measurable loan assets which have been sanctioned (the 'DLG set')

requirements.full_sentence:
This
portfolio shall remain fixed for the purpose of DLG cover and is not meant to be
dynamic.
requirements.object: purpose of DLG cover and is not meant to be dynamic

requirements.full_sentence:
This
portfolio shall remain "frozen" for the purpose of the specific DLG arrangement
- meaning that no loan assets can be added or removed from it, except through
loan repayment/ write-off.
requirements.object: purpose of the specific DLG arrangement - meaning that no loan assets can be added or removed from it

requirements.full_sentence:
The ceiling for DLG cover on such portfolio shall be fixed at ₹2 crore (5 per cent
of ₹40 crore), which shall get activated proportionately as and when the loans
are disbursed.
requirements.object: crore (5 per cent of ₹40 crore), which shall get activated proportionately as and when the loans are disbursed

requirements.full_sentence:
Then as on April 1, 2024, the DLG cover available for
the portfolio shall be ₹0.5 crore (5 per cent of disbursed).

conditions: if loans of ₹10 crore are further disbursed on April 15
requirements.full_sentence:
Subsequently, if loans of ₹10 crore are further disbursed on April 15, 2024, the
DLG cover shall proportionately increase to ₹1 crore effective April 15, 2024.
requirements.object: crore effective April 15, 2024

requirements.full_sentence:
In this case, the outstanding portfolio in the books of the bank would be ₹15
crore and the DLG cover shall remain at ₹1 crore.

the liability of the borrowers in respect of the underlying loan shall
remain unaffected.

(4) DLG amount once invoked by a bank shall not be reinstated, including through

Treatment of DLG for regulatory capital
(1) Capital computation, i.e., computation of exposure and application of Credit Risk
Mitigation benefits on individual loan assets in the portfolio shall continue to be
governed by the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025.

(2) In case, DLG provider is a bank, it shall deduct full amount of the DLG which is
outstanding from its capital.

Invocation and tenor of DLG
(1) A bank shall invoke DLG within a maximum overdue period of 120 days, unless
the loan dues are made good by the borrower before that.
requirements.object: maximum overdue period of 120 days

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The period for which the DLG agreement remains in force shall not be less than
the longest tenor of the loan in the underlying loan portfolio.
requirements.object: longest tenor of the loan in the underlying loan portfolio

requirements.full_sentence:
Disclosure requirements
(1) A bank shall put in place a mechanism to ensure that LSPs with whom they
have a DLG arrangement shall publish on their website the total number of
portfolios and the respective amount of each portfolio on which DLG has been
offered.

The name of the lender(s) may or may not be disclosed as part of
disclosure under this provision.
requirements.object: disclosed as part of disclosure under this provision

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Disclosure under para (1) above shall be made on a monthly basis, with the
disclosure for any given month to be provided no later than seven (7) working
days following the conclusion of that month.

Exceptions
Guarantees covered under the following schemes/ entities shall not be covered
within the definition of DLG:
(1) Guarantee schemes of Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small
Enterprises (CGTMSE), Credit Risk Guarantee Fund Trust for Low Income
Housing (CRGFTLIH) and individual schemes under National Credit Guarantee
Trustee Company Ltd (NCGTC).
requirements.object: definition of DLG: (1) Guarantee schemes of Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE), Credit Risk Gu

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) These Directions shall also be applicable to all loans offered on Debit Card,
including EMI programmes.
requirements.object: all loans offered on Debit Card, including EMI programmes

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) DLG arrangements entered between the bank and LSP conforming to the
instructions laid down in these Directions shall not attract the provisions of 'loan
participation' as defined under the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
Banks - Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: 'loan participation' as defined under the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Transfer and Distribution of Cre

requirements.full_sentence:
The credit policy (hereinafter called the policy) of a bank, as required in terms of
paragraph 5 of these Directions, shall include, inter alia, appropriate single borrower
limits and aggregate limits for the portfolio of loans against collateral of jewellery,
ornaments or coins made of gold or silver ("eligible collateral" for this Chapter);
requirements.object: single borrower limits and aggregate limits for the portfolio of loans against collateral of jewellery, ornaments or

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy shall also include appropriate documentation to be obtained and
maintained for loans proposed to be categorised under priority sector lending.
requirements.object: to be obtained and maintained for loans proposed to be categorised under priority sector lending

requirements.full_sentence: A bank may decide on a suitable approach for lending against eligible collateral as
requirements.object: approach for lending against eligible collateral as

However, detailed credit
assessment, including assessment of borrower's repayment capacity shall be
undertaken in case the total loan amount against eligible collateral is above ₹2.5
lakh to a borrower.
requirements.object: the total loan amount against eligible collateral is above ₹2

conditions: Provided that in case of Bullet repayment loans
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that in case of Bullet repayment loans, the threshold loan amount for
detailed credit assessment shall be the total amount payable at maturity.
requirements.object: payable at maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may renew an existing loan or sanction a top-up loan upon a formal request
from the borrower, it shall be subject to a credit assessment in accordance with
above paragraph 33.
requirements.object: credit assessment in accordance with above paragraph 33

requirements.full_sentence:
Such renewal or top-up shall be permitted only within the
permissible LTV, and provided the loan is classified as standard.
requirements.object: the permissible LTV, and provided the loan is classified as standard

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, renewal
of bullet repayment loan shall be allowed only after payment of accrued interest, if
any.
requirements.object: payment of accrued interest

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall ensure that such renewals and top-ups are clearly identifiable in
its Core Banking System or Loan Processing System.
requirements.object: and top-ups are clearly identifiable in its Core Banking System or Loan Processing System

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not grant any advance or loan:
(1) For purchase of gold in any form including primary gold, ornaments, jewellery,
or coins, or for purchase of financial assets backed by gold, e.g., units of
Exchange-traded funds (ETFs) or units of Mutual Funds, and
(2) against primary gold or silver or financial assets backed by primary gold or silver.
requirements.object: loan: (1) For purchase of gold in any form including primary gold, ornaments, jewellery, or coins, or for purchase of financial a

requirements.full_sentence: A bank shall not extend a loan where ownership of the collateral is doubtful.
requirements.object: ownership of the collateral is doubtful

requirements.full_sentence:
A
suitable document or declaration shall be obtained from the borrower in all cases to
the effect that the borrower is the rightful owner of the eligible collateral.
requirements.object: borrower in all cases to the effect that the borrower is the rightful owner of the eligible collateral

requirements.full_sentence:
Multiple or
frequent sanction of loans against eligible collateral to the same borrower,
aggregating to a value in excess of a threshold to be decided by the lender, must
be examined closely as part of the transaction monitoring under the anti-money
laundering (AML) framework.
requirements.object: part of the transaction monitoring under the anti-money laundering (AML) framework

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not:
(1) Avail loans by re-pledging gold or silver pledged to it by its borrowers.
requirements.object: by re-pledging gold or silver pledged to it by its borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
Tenor of consumption loans in the nature of bullet repayment loans shall be capped
at 12 months, which may be renewed in terms of paragraph 34.
requirements.object: months, which may be renewed in terms of paragraph 34

requirements.full_sentence:
Loans against ornaments and coins shall be subject to the following:
(1) the aggregate weight of ornaments pledged for all loans to a borrower shall not
exceed 1 kilogram for gold ornaments, and 10 kilograms for silver ornaments.
requirements.object: gold ornaments, and 10 kilograms for silver ornaments

conditions: in case of gold coins
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) the aggregate weight of coin(s) pledged for all loans to a borrower shall not
exceed 50 grams in case of gold coins, and 500 grams in case of silver coins.
requirements.object: case of gold coins, and 500 grams in case of silver coins

requirements.full_sentence:
Gold or silver accepted as collateral shall be valued based on the reference price
corresponding to its actual purity (caratage).
requirements.object: the reference price corresponding to its actual purity (caratage)

requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, the lower of
(1) the average closing price for gold or silver, as the case may be, of that specific
purity over the preceding 30 days, or
(2) the closing price for gold or silver, as the case may be, of that specific purity on
the preceding day, as published either by the India Bullion and Jewellers
Association Ltd.
requirements.object: purity over the preceding 30 days, or (2) the closing price for gold or silver, as the case may be, of that specific purity on th

requirements.full_sentence:
(IBJA) or by a commodity exchange regulated by the Securities
and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) shall be used.

conditions: If price information for the specific purity is not directly available
requirements.full_sentence:
If price information for the specific purity is not directly available, the lender shall use
the published price available for the nearest available purity and proportionately
adjust the weight of the collateral based on its actual purity to arrive at valuation.
requirements.object: available for the nearest available purity and proportionately adjust the weight of the collateral based on its actual purity

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of valuation, only the intrinsic value of the gold or silver contained
in the eligible collateral shall be reckoned and no other cost elements, such as
precious stones or gems, shall be added thereto.
requirements.object: other cost elements, such as precious stones or gems, shall be added thereto

requirements.object: ongoing basis throughout the tenor of the loan

Customer) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Know Your Customer) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
Provisions of Sections 269 SS and 269 T of the Income
Tax Act, 1961, and associated rules shall be complied with, as may be applicable.
requirements.object: may be applicable

conditions: In case of bank transfers
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of bank transfers, the bank shall ensure that:
(1) Loan disbursals are made to the borrower's account and not to a third-party
account.
requirements.object: disbursals are made to the borrower's account and not to a third-party account

requirements.full_sentence:
Running multiple loans simultaneously to a single borrower or a group of related
borrowers may be prone to misuse and susceptible to fraud.
requirements.object: and susceptible to fraud

requirements.full_sentence:
Consequently, such
practices shall be subject to stricter internal audit and supervisory examination.
requirements.object: internal audit and supervisory examination

requirements.full_sentence:
For instructions on disclosures, banks shall be guided by the instructions contained
in Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Financial Statements:
Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: instructions contained in Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Direc

requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, the household shall
mean an individual family unit, i.e., husband, wife and their unmarried children.
requirements.object: unit, i

requirements.full_sentence:
All collateral-free loans, irrespective of end use and mode of application/ processing/
disbursal (either through physical or digital channels), provided to low-income
households, i.e., households having annual income up to ₹3,00,000, shall be
considered as microfinance loans.
requirements.object: loans

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: To ensure collateral-free nature of the microfinance loan, the loan shall
not be linked with a lien on the deposit account of the borrower or backed by
hypothecation of any security.
requirements.object: lien on the deposit account of the borrower or backed by hypothecation of any security

Each bank shall put in place a board-approved policy for assessment of household
income.

Indicative methodology for assessment of household income is outlined
below:
(1) For undertaking the income assessment of a low-income household, information
related to following parameters may be captured by the lender:

(b) Irregular expenses over last one year (medical expenses, house

requirements.object: household income for the purpose of calculating the indebtedness of the household

requirements.object: outstanding loans (collateral-free microfinance loans as well as any other type of collateralized loans) of the household

requirements.object: principal as well as interest component) towards all existing loans as well as the loan under consideration

requirements.full_sentence:

obligations of a household as a percentage of the monthly household income
exceed the limit of 50 per cent, shall be allowed to mature.

However, in such cases,
no new loans shall be provided to these households till the prescribed limit of 50 per

requirements.object: households till the prescribed limit of 50 per cent is complied with

requirements.full_sentence:
Each bank shall provide timely and accurate data to the CICs and use the data
available with them to ensure compliance with the level of indebtedness.
requirements.object: data to the CICs and use the data available with them to ensure compliance with the level of indebtedness

requirements.full_sentence:
Besides,

the borrowers, their bank account statements and local enquiries.
requirements.object: from other sources such as declaration from the borrowers, their bank account statements and local enquiries

requirements.full_sentence:

requirements.object: to provide the flexibility of repayment periodicity on microfinance loans as per borrowers' requirement

(1) Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Interest Rates on Advances)
Directions, 2025 for pricing of loans.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Interest Rates on Advances) Directions, 2025 for pricing of loans

conditions: where forthcoming
requirements.full_sentence:
RCBs may secure housing loans either by mortgage of property or by government
guarantee where forthcoming, or by both.
requirements.object: by mortgage of property or by government guarantee where forthcoming, or by both

conditions: Where this is not feasible
requirements.full_sentence:
Where this is not feasible, banks may
accept security of adequate value in the form of LIC policies, Government
promissory Notes, shares / debentures, gold ornaments or such other security as
they deem appropriate.
requirements.object: value in the form of LIC policies, Government promissory Notes, shares / debentures, gold ornaments or such other security

The instalments should be fixed on a realistic basis taking into account

requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, the outgo on account of
housing loan repayment (including principal and interest) should not normally
exceed 30 per cent of the borrower's income.
requirements.object: cent of the borrower's income

requirements.object: quarterly instalments on a graduated basis

requirements.full_sentence:

/ or against such other security as they may deem appropriate.
requirements.object: pari passu or second mortgage charge over the property mortgaged in favour of other lenders and / or against such other

requirements.full_sentence:

requirements.object: upto a maximum of ₹ 1 lakh to owner of houses / flats for repairs, additions, alterations, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
They should satisfy themselves regarding the estimated cost of repairs, additions,
etc.
requirements.object: estimated cost of repairs, additions, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
RCBs may extend term loans to Housing Boards within their State, other
governmental agencies or non-governmental agencies approved by National
Housing Bank / NABARD for the purpose of refinance, provided that not less than
75 per cent of the units financed by the loan are meant for EWS and LIG.
requirements.object: cent of the units financed by the loan are meant for EWS and LIG

requirements.full_sentence:
In order to develop a healthy housing finance system, while extending such loans
banks may not only keep in view the past performance of these Boards / agencies
in the matter or recovery from the beneficiaries but should also stipulate that the
Boards / agencies will ensure prompt and regular recovery of loan instalments from

requirements.object: the past performance of these Boards / agencies in the matter or recovery from the beneficiaries but should also stipulate that the

requirements.full_sentence:
While financing housing schemes in participation with other co-operative banks or
commercial banks, it should be ensured that the terms and conditions of such
Act and the rules framed
participation arrangements are in conformity with the provisions of the Co-operative
Societies Act and the rules framed thereunder.
requirements.object: terms and conditions of such Act and the rules framed participation arrangements are in conformity with the provisions of the Co-o

requirements.full_sentence:
The criteria for classification of borrowers under EWS, LIG and MIG shall be in
conformity with the norms laid down by the Government of India.
requirements.object: the norms laid down by the Government of India

requirements.full_sentence:

/ builders on behalf of individual borrowers to banks may lead to lower credit rating

about servicing of loans gets passed on to the CICs on a regular basis.
requirements.object: rating / scoring of such borrowers by credit information companies (CICs) as information about servicing of loans gets passed on

requirements.full_sentence:

housing loans and customer suitability issues, the banks are advised that disbursal
of housing loans sanctioned to individuals shall be closely linked to the stages of
construction of the housing project / houses and upfront disbursal shall not be made
in cases of incomplete / under-construction / green field housing projects.
requirements.object: of incomplete / under-construction / green field housing projects

It is emphasized that the banks while introducing any kind of product shall take into
account the customer suitability and appropriateness issues and also ensure that
the borrowers / customers are made fully aware of the risks and liabilities under
such products.
requirements.object: customer suitability and appropriateness issues and also ensure that the borrowers / customers are made fully aware of the risks

For this purpose, CRE-RH shall consist of loans to builders / developers for

Such projects should
ordinarily not include non-residential commercial real estate.

shopping complex,
school, etc.) can also be classified under CRE-RH, provided that the commercial

within 3 months of completion
of construction

where the applicant approaches the bank for a credit facility to

but which the applicant intends to use for commercial purposes and declares so
while applying for loan.
requirements.object: respect of properties meant for residential use, but which the applicant intends to use for commercial purposes and declares so while

requirements.full_sentence:
Within this Chapter, the term Regulated Entity (RE) or Regulated Entities (REs) shall
refer to:
(1) Commercial Banks (including Regional Rural Banks and Local Area Banks).
requirements.object: Banks (including Regional Rural Banks and Local Area Banks)

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions in this Chapter shall not apply to the derivative exposures of the bank
(wherever permitted under specific regulatory framework issued by RBI) other than
the General Conditions as laid down in this Chapter.
requirements.object: exposures of the bank (wherever permitted under specific regulatory framework issued by RBI) other than the General Conditions

requirements.full_sentence:
The credit policy of the bank shall incorporate suitable provisions for issue of NFB
facilities, inter alia, covering aspects relating to type of NFB facilities, limits granted,
credit appraisal, security requirement, fraud prevention, overall monitoring
mechanism including post-sanction monitoring, delegation matrix, audit and internal
controls, compliance to uniform standards issued by standard setting bodies and
other safeguards.
requirements.object: issue of NFB facilities, inter alia, covering aspects relating to type of NFB facilities, limits granted, credit a

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall issue a NFB facility only on behalf of a customer having funded credit
facility from the bank.
requirements.object: only on behalf of a customer having funded credit facility from the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that this clause shall not be applicable in respect of:
(1) Derivative contracts entered into by the bank with counterparty, if applicable.
requirements.object: of: (1) Derivative contracts entered into by the bank with counterparty

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The eligible financial collateral specified herein shall be as defined
under Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital
Adequacy) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall not issue a NFB facility to any entity assuring redemption / repayment
of funds raised by any entity via deposits, issuance of bonds, or in any other form,
unless specifically permitted under any regulatory guidelines / directions issued by
the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: to any entity assuring redemption / repayment of funds raised by any entity via deposits, issuance of bonds, or in any other form

requirements.full_sentence:
Once a NFB facility devolves and is converted into a fund based facility, then the
prudential norms shall be as applicable to fund based facilities.
requirements.object: fund based facilities

requirements.full_sentence:
In general, a guarantee (or a counter-guarantee) issued by the bank (guarantor)
shall be irrevocable (i.e., there shall be no clause in the contract that would allow
the guarantor to unilaterally cancel the same), unconditional (i.e.

there shall be no
clause in the contract that could prevent the bank from being obliged to pay out in a
timely manner in the event that the original counterparty fails to meet its obligation),
incontrovertible and shall contain a clear mechanism for honouring the same without
demur as and when invoked.
requirements.object: the contract that could prevent the bank from being obliged to pay out in a timely manner in the event that the original counterparty

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall put in place suitable internal aggregate / individual ceilings for
issuance of guarantees in general and unsecured guarantees in particular.
requirements.object: internal aggregate / individual ceilings for issuance of guarantees in general and unsecured guarantees in particular

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the total volume of guaranteed obligations of StCBs and CCBs
outstanding at any time shall not exceed 5 per cent of their total assets as per the
previous financial year's balance sheet.
requirements.object: of their total assets as per the previous financial year's balance sheet

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, unsecured guarantees of banks
shall be restricted to 1.25 per cent of total assets.

Any such bank in breach of the
above stipulation of these Directions shall meet the above threshold by April 01,
2027.
requirements.object: by April 01, 2027

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of the internal policy relating to guarantees shall, inter alia, address
aspects related to invocation and settlement mechanism, claim period, tenor, fee /
commission / applicable charges, timelines for release of security, renewal, fraud
prevention measures etc.
requirements.object: aspects related to invocation and settlement mechanism, claim period, tenor, fee / commission / applicable charges, timelines fo

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall honour the guarantee issued by it as and when invoked in
accordance with the terms and conditions of the guarantee deed unless there is
court order restraining the same
B.1 Usage of electronic-Guarantee
requirements.object: by it as and when invoked in accordance with the terms and conditions of the guarantee deed unless there is court order re

requirements.full_sentence:
Wherever the bank issues an electronic Guarantee, it shall frame a standard
operating procedure (SOP) aimed at minimization of manual intervention;
requirements.object: procedure (SOP) aimed at minimization of manual intervention

requirements.full_sentence:
The SOP shall, inter alia, consider the aspects mentioned at paragraphs 91 to
104 of these Directions.
requirements.object: the aspects mentioned at paragraphs 91 to 104 of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall have suitable enabling provisions in its credit policy which shall, inter
alia, envisage the adoption of electronic Guarantees, the risk controls to be put in
place, delegation of authority, the monitoring process, etc.
requirements.object: in its credit policy which shall, inter alia, envisage the adoption of electronic Guarantees, the risk controls to b

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall put in place appropriate SOPs for user reference, detailing all the
steps to be followed during the entire electronic Guarantee lifecycle.
requirements.object: SOPs for user reference, detailing all the steps to be followed during the entire electronic Guarantee lifecycle

requirements.full_sentence:
Electronic
Guarantees shall not be issued without ensuring that the underlying transaction has
been duly reflected in the Core Banking System (CBS)/ Trade Finance System
(TFS).
requirements.object: that the underlying transaction has been duly reflected in the Core Banking System (CBS)/ Trade Finance System (TFS)

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall have a strong control environment covering the policies, processes
and systems;
requirements.object: environment covering the policies, processes and systems

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall ensure that all features relating to the entire lifecycle events of
electronic Guarantees such as issuance, amendment, invocation, cancellation etc.
requirements.object: relating to the entire lifecycle events of electronic Guarantees such as issuance, amendment, invocation, cancellation etc

requirements.full_sentence:
shall be available on its platform through suitable integration with the electronic
Guarantee service provider.
requirements.object: platform through suitable integration with the electronic Guarantee service provider

requirements.full_sentence:
B.4 The CBS / TFS shall be integrated with the APIs and other related
messaging platforms offered by the electronic Guarantee service provider, in
Straight Through Processing (STP) mode, without any manual intervention.
requirements.object: APIs and other related messaging platforms offered by the electronic Guarantee service provider, in Straight Through Processing

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall have an efficient system of 'Maker, Checker and Authorizer' for
issuance and monitoring of electronic Guarantees, while ensuring strict access
control and an effective segregation of the role and accountability.
requirements.object: of 'Maker, Checker and Authorizer' for issuance and monitoring of electronic Guarantees, while ensuring strict access control

requirements.full_sentence:
No role involved in electronic Guarantee issuance lifecycle shall violate principle of
segregation of duties, four / six eye principle and no employee shall be allocated
roles / privileges across systems, applications that are conflicting in nature or in
violation of four/ six eye principle.
requirements.object: of duties, four / six eye principle and no employee shall be allocated roles / privileges across systems, application

requirements.full_sentence:
The system access shall be provided only to specified users, and access through
generic user IDs shall not be permitted.

User review shall be continuous, at defined
periodicity and identifiable at any point of time with respective rights and privileges.
requirements.object: periodicity and identifiable at any point of time with respective rights and privileges

requirements.full_sentence: The user privileges shall be decided on "need to know/ need to do" basis.
requirements.object: to know/ need to do" basis

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall have in place a system of periodical review and reconciliation of all
the electronic Guarantees issued / modified / cancelled, during the specified period.
requirements.object: system of periodical review and reconciliation of all the electronic Guarantees issued / modified / cancelled, during the specified pe

requirements.full_sentence:
The issuance of electronic Guarantees shall be mandatorily covered within the
scope of concurrent audit and RBIA of the bank.
requirements.object: the scope of concurrent audit and RBIA of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The robustness of the electronic Guarantee systems shall be part of the
Vulnerability Assessment / Penetration Testing (VA / PT), Information Systems
Audit.
requirements.object: Vulnerability Assessment / Penetration Testing (VA / PT), Information Systems Audit

requirements.full_sentence: Dependence on the vendors for day-to-day transactions shall be avoided.

Access to production systems shall be provided to vendors only in a controlled
environment, and audit trail shall be maintained.
requirements.object: only in a controlled environment, and audit trail shall be maintained

requirements.full_sentence:
Security Incident and Event Management (SIEM) tool shall be integrated with the
concerned servers and consoles / PCs connected to electronic Guarantee related
critical systems directly in its VLAN to generate automatic alerts.
requirements.object: concerned servers and consoles / PCs connected to electronic Guarantee related critical systems directly in its VLAN to generat

The bank shall integrate electronic Guarantee systems with Privileged User
Management Systems / Identity and Access Management systems.
requirements.object: with Privileged User Management Systems / Identity and Access Management systems

requirements.full_sentence:
The logs of the
same shall be monitored through Security Operation Centre (SOC) setup.
requirements.object: Operation Centre (SOC) setup

requirements.full_sentence:
Business Continuity Measures and contingency plans for system failures, shall
be put in place by the bank.
requirements.object: by the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall, not provide a guarantee favouring another RE to enable it to
provide any fund based credit facility to an obligor unless specifically permitted by
RBI.
requirements.object: guarantee favouring another RE to enable it to provide any fund based credit facility to an obligor unless specifically permitted by R

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that this clause shall not be applicable in case of credit facilities extended
against guarantees pertaining to trade related transactions.
requirements.object: of credit facilities extended against guarantees pertaining to trade related transactions

However, the bank may provide a guarantee favouring another RE for a NFB
facility extended by the latter.
requirements.object: another RE for a NFB facility extended by the latter

requirements.full_sentence:
Such guarantee issued by a RE shall be treated as
an exposure on the obligor on whose behalf the guarantee has been issued by it,
for all purposes including for the calculation of capital adequacy.
requirements.object: exposure on the obligor on whose behalf the guarantee has been issued by it, for all purposes including for the calculation of capita

requirements.object: claim / exposure on the RE which is providing the counter guarantee

Only genuine trade bills shall be co-accepted, and it shall be ensured that the

the borrowers.
requirements.object: shall be ensured that the goods covered by bills co-accepted are actually received in the stock accounts of the borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
Proper records of the bills co-accepted for each customer shall be maintained,
so that the commitments for each customer and the total commitments at a branch
can be readily ascertained, and these shall be part of internal audit.
requirements.object: the commitments for each customer and the total commitments at a branch can be readily ascertained, and these shall be part of

seller has received funding for the underlying trade transaction from any RE.
requirements.object: another RE or where the buyer / seller has received funding for the underlying trade transaction from any RE

requirements.full_sentence:
The banks permitted as Authorized Dealer (AD) may extend NFB facilities as
permitted under the extant regulations / Directions issued under Foreign Exchange
Management Act, 1999, for bonafide current or capital account transaction,
including guarantees in respect of debt or other liability incurred by an exporter on

banks

For disclosure of the details of NFB credit facilities, the bank shall be guided by

requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions, 2025

FEMA

The bank may grant advances against the security of National Savings
Certificates subject to normal terms and conditions.

However, the banks shall
prescribe a margin of 25 per cent on the value of original investment in National

advances to the extent of 75 per cent of such value.

Vikas Patras.

The banks shall maintain minimum
margin of 50 per cent on loans to individuals against equity shares.
requirements.object: 50 per cent on loans to individuals against equity shares

The RCBs shall not grant loans to Non-Residents without obtaining clearance
from the Foreign Exchange Department of RBI.
requirements.object: without obtaining clearance from the Foreign Exchange Department of RBI

Though the need for obtaining prior authorisation from NABARD was dispensed
with effect from April 24, 2000, in respect of loans sanctioned to the co-operative
marketing societies / consumer stores / societies / State level co-operative
federations under purchase / procurement schemes of State Governments outside
PDS, beyond certain limits, an RCB shall obtain the prior authorisation from

Accordingly, the State/Central cooperative banks may reduce the margin in respect

when State and Central Co-
operative Banks are members of a consortia with other Commercial Banks
requirements.full_sentence:
However, in cases when State and Central Co-
operative Banks are members of a consortia with other Commercial Banks, the
borrowers may be covered by instructions applicable in respect of credit limits in

In such cases the State and Central
Co-operative Banks should follow the same financial discipline as may be stipulated

requirements.object: members of the consortium

requirements.full_sentence:
The operating instructions to give effect to the
decision are set out below:
(1) In the case of advances under consortium arrangements, the credit facilities for
financing inland credit sales should first be segregated from the overall
permissible limit enjoyed by a borrower from the consortium.
requirements.object: the overall permissible limit enjoyed by a borrower from the consortium

requirements.full_sentence:
Each member of
the consortium should be advised of its share of the said facility (for financing
inland credit sales) within the limit allocated to it.
requirements.object: share of the said facility (for financing inland credit sales) within the limit allocated to it

requirements.full_sentence:
Thereafter, the limit allocated
to each member for inland credit sales should be segregated in respect of bills
and book debts.
requirements.object: of bills and book debts

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The limit sanctioned against book debts should not be more than 75 per cent of
the aggregate limits sanctioned to such a borrower for financing his inland credit
sales.
requirements.object: per cent of the aggregate limits sanctioned to such a borrower for financing his inland credit sales

requirements.full_sentence:
The aggregate limits referred to above will comprise all types of credit
facilities extended towards inland credit sales such as cash credit limit/sub-limit
against book debts, bills purchased/discounted limits, overdraft/cash credit
limit/sub-limit against bills sent for collection, and similar limit/sub-limit against
Government supply bills, as may have been made available within the overall
bank finance limit sanctioned.
requirements.object: within the overall bank finance limit sanctioned

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Each member bank should levy the additional interest of 2 per cent on the
portion of the book-debt finance in excess of 75 per cent of the limit
allocated/fixed for financing inland credit sales.
requirements.object: of 2 per cent on the portion of the book-debt finance in excess of 75 per cent of the limit allocated/fixed for financing

conditions: In case of advances granted under consortium arrangements
requirements.full_sentence:
(5) In case of advances granted under consortium arrangements, the State and
Central Co-operative Banks shall note for their guidance that the instructions
issued from time to time on the subject to Commercial Banks will equally be
applicable to State and Central Cooperative Banks which are members of the
consortium.
requirements.object: that the instructions issued from time to time on the subject to Commercial Banks will equally be applicable to State and Cent

requirements.full_sentence:
Trade Relief Measures
To mitigate the burden of debt servicing brought about by trade disruptions caused
by global headwinds and to ensure the continuity of viable businesses, banks
extending export credit finance may provide relief measures to eligible borrowers,
as specified under Reserve Bank of India (Trade Relief Measures) Directions, 2025
dated November 14, 2025.
requirements.object: eligible borrowers, as specified under Reserve Bank of India (Trade Relief Measures) Directions, 2025 dated November 14, 20

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions,
instructions and guidelines already repealed shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue
to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the repeal of these directions, instructions, or guidelines shall not in any
way prejudicially affect:
(1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder.
requirements.object: affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

conditions: if those directions
requirements.full_sentence:
and
any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:

to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of these
Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
